reasoning: The prompt asks whether the 6-month Treasury yield will have increased by the resolution date (presumed 2026-01-24) compared to its value on 2025-10-26. Over a ~3-month horizon, changes in short-term government bond yields closely resemble a random walk as current market expectations are already priced into forward rates. Without specific macroeconomic details for late 2025, a base rate close to 50% is the most appropriate anchor. I apply a slight downward bias to 49% to account for a scenario where inflation normalization leads to gradual central bank easing (which corresponds to a 3.8% rate assumption), slightly favoring a decline or stagnant yield.
